disclaimer: Educational assessment. CARSI is an IICRC-approved CEC provider; this is CARSI-original Australian training, not IICRC certification.
note: Australian-produced. Grounded in the course's own modules. Founder to review before wiring live.

quizzes.title: Documenting and Reporting Air Quality Improvements — Course Quiz

questions.questionText: According to the course, monitoring air quality is described as an especially essential part of the remediation process when dealing with which situations?
options: Only during the final handover of a completed project
Electrical rewiring and cabling tasks
Remediation involving water damage, fire damage, or mould contamination
Outdoor landscaping and groundwork
questions.sourceRef: M1 — Understanding Air Quality and Its Importance in Restoration

questions.questionText: Which contaminant does the course describe as being released when mould is disturbed during water or fire damage work and able to spread rapidly in enclosed spaces?
options: Mould spores
Asbestos fibres
Carbon monoxide
Nitrogen dioxide
questions.sourceRef: M2 — Common Contaminants Found on Job Sites

questions.questionText: The course states that prolonged exposure to high levels of VOCs can have which serious long-term effects?
options: Mesothelioma and asbestosis
Liver damage, kidney damage, or cancer
Immediate improvement in respiratory function
A reduction in job-site humidity
questions.sourceRef: M2 — Common Contaminants Found on Job Sites

questions.questionText: Which instrument does the course recommend for measuring the presence of VOCs and other gases in real-time?
options: A particulate counter
An airborne mould testing kit
A temperature and humidity sensor
A Photoionisation Detector (PID)
questions.sourceRef: M3 — Tools and Equipment for Monitoring Air Quality

questions.questionText: According to the course, what does a particulate counter measure?
options: The concentration, number and size of dust and particulate matter in the air
The presence of VOCs and solvent vapours
Carbon monoxide and nitrogen dioxide levels
The species of airborne mould spores present
questions.sourceRef: M3 — Tools and Equipment for Monitoring Air Quality

questions.questionText: For protection against particulate matter, which respirator does the course identify as suitable?
options: A full-face respirator with a particulate and gas filter
An N95 respirator
A lightweight cloth face covering
No respirator is required for particulate matter
questions.sourceRef: M4 — Safety Measures and Protocols for Handling Poor Air Quality

questions.questionText: What is the stated purpose of a negative air machine on a job site?
options: To measure VOC concentrations in real-time
To heat the affected area and speed up drying
To create a negative pressure environment that prevents contaminants from spreading to other parts of the building
To automatically record air quality readings for reports
questions.sourceRef: M4 — Safety Measures and Protocols for Handling Poor Air Quality

questions.questionText: Why does the course say documenting and reporting air quality is important?
options: It is only needed to market the restoration business
It removes the need to monitor air quality with equipment
It is optional and has no bearing on regulatory compliance
It helps ensure compliance with health and safety regulations, provides data for insurance claims, and can serve as evidence in disputes
questions.sourceRef: M5 — Documenting and Reporting Air Quality on Site
